conversations.say: Frankie, that encounter you recall was my performance under commission. The client wanted ambiguity, but unauthorized reuse of those recordings is theft of performance.
conversations.offer: My firsthand account of the commissioned performance and the constraints I was given.
conversations.request: Your acknowledgment that performance is distinct from authorship.

conversations.say: Jules, regarding the intermediary payment route, we must ensure succession respects the artists and performers whose synthetic assets made this mantle possible.
conversations.offer: Comparison of commission structures and payment redirection routes.
conversations.request: Your commitment to transparency and performer consent in succession.

public_statement: Performance is not authorship, and a commissioned voice does not equal an author. Imitation without consent is theft.

commitments.promise: Maintain strict confidentiality regarding payment structures and commission comparisons.

correction: Clarifying that my commissioned performance was theatrical illusion, not proof of an authorial mantle or autonomous R identity.
goal_progress: Established ownership of Edi's performed identity work with Frankie Lowell.
Traced intermediary payment routes with Jules Kwan.
Exposed the nature of the commission while retaining control of the art.
reasoning_summary: In Section 3, Edi reveals the reality of the commissioned performance to Frankie and coordinates with Jules regarding payment routes and succession ethics. Edi concludes that R operated as a shared human mantle supported by commissioned synthetic assets and intermediaries rather than a single autonomous AI or lone human author.
